title: Reverse-engineered persona
notes: Both approaches achieve perfect groundedness scores (1.0) on the deterministic check. The metric is saturated at ceiling — it validates record ID references and field_path coverage, not semantic depth. The transcript-first approach produces qualitatively richer customer voice (more specific quotes, 2am incident detail, rate-per-hour framing) but this is invisible to the current metric. To detect the hypothesized advantage, a semantic groundedness metric (e.g. claim-to-evidence cosine similarity) is needed.
